driftClosure.policy: Every release evidence item must map to a producer job that is either a release prerequisite or release job artifact, and every consumed artifact must stay visible in the governance dashboard before tag promotion.

tagCIClosure.policy: Version tag promotion must connect release snapshot, artifact integrity, container digest, vulnerability scan, provenance attestation, required checks, dashboard evidence, publish decision, block decision, and rollback action before adopters can mirror or deploy a release.

stages.closureInvariant: Tag CI must build the release snapshot, archives, checksums, and archive SBOM from the tagged commit before any publish decision is allowed.
stages.publishDecision: Continue only when release snapshot artifacts are reproducible from the tagged commit.
stages.blockDecision: Block promotion when snapshot, checksum, or archive SBOM output is missing or not traceable to the tag.
stages.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous release pinned and rebuild the snapshot from the tagged commit after fixing the release configuration.

stages.closureInvariant: Archive and container SBOM evidence must stay paired with checksums so adopters can audit shipped dependency inventory before mirroring artifacts.
stages.publishDecision: Publish archives and image metadata only when checksum and SBOM evidence pass the local release artifact gate.
stages.blockDecision: Block release publication when checksum verification or SBOM evidence is absent, empty, or unexplained.
stages.rollbackOrEscalation: Reject the new artifact set and keep using the previous release until integrity and SBOM evidence are rebuilt.

stages.closureInvariant: Release containers must be deployable by immutable digest and backed by Trivy scan output plus checksum and Docker provenance attestations.
stages.publishDecision: Promote only immutable image digests with passing Trivy and attestation evidence.
stages.blockDecision: Block image promotion when digest, Trivy, checksum attestation, or Docker attestation evidence is missing or mismatched.
stages.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous known-good digest and rebuild or re-attest the release image from the tagged commit.

stages.closureInvariant: Required tag checks, branch protection expectations, release prerequisites, and dashboard evidence must stay aligned before tag promotion.
stages.publishDecision: Allow publication only when API compatibility, security, race, benchmark, and governance dashboard evidence are release-blocking and visible.
stages.blockDecision: Block promotion when any required check is skipped, removed from tag prerequisites, or missing from the governance dashboard.
stages.rollbackOrEscalation: Restore the missing check or pin the previous release until required-check drift and dashboard evidence are closed.

items.questionAnswered: Do downloaded archives match the release manifest?
items.consumerAction: verify checksums before installing or mirroring archives
items.rollbackOrEscalation: reject the release artifact and rebuild from the tagged commit

items.questionAnswered: What dependency and file inventory is shipped in each archive?
items.consumerAction: review SBOM deltas before approving a dependency-sensitive upgrade
items.rollbackOrEscalation: pin the previous release until unexpected SBOM entries are explained

items.questionAnswered: What dependency inventory is shipped in the release container?
items.consumerAction: review container SBOM before promoting the image
items.rollbackOrEscalation: keep the previous image digest pinned until the SBOM delta is accepted

items.questionAnswered: Were archive checksums covered by provenance attestation?
items.consumerAction: verify attestation issuer and subject before distributing archives
items.rollbackOrEscalation: block publication when attestation verification is missing or mismatched

items.questionAnswered: Was the release container covered by provenance attestation?
items.consumerAction: verify image attestation before deployment promotion
items.rollbackOrEscalation: continue running the previously attested digest

items.questionAnswered: Which immutable image digests should production pin?
items.consumerAction: deploy by digest rather than mutable tag
items.rollbackOrEscalation: roll back to the previous known-good digest

items.questionAnswered: Does the release container have known vulnerability findings?
items.consumerAction: review reachable high or critical findings before production rollout
items.rollbackOrEscalation: block promotion or document mitigation before override

items.questionAnswered: Did public API, CLI, RPC, or generated contracts break compatibility?
items.consumerAction: inspect breaking changes before upgrading generated projects
items.rollbackOrEscalation: pin the previous gofly version or require migration notes

items.questionAnswered: Did govulncheck or gosec detect release-blocking issues?
items.consumerAction: review new findings and confirm no unexplained baseline expansion
items.rollbackOrEscalation: block release until the finding is fixed or documented as unreachable

items.questionAnswered: Did race-enabled tests pass for concurrent runtime paths?
items.consumerAction: review race failures before enabling new runtime or RPC paths
items.rollbackOrEscalation: disable the new path or pin the previous release until fixed

items.consumerAction: check blocking allocation or latency budget failures before rollout
items.rollbackOrEscalation: hold promotion or downgrade affected hot-path feature usage

items.questionAnswered: Is the release-ready governance summary complete?
items.consumerAction: review readiness score, API tiers, coverage, security, benchmark, and aiflow status
items.rollbackOrEscalation: do not publish until the dashboard reports ready status
